title: quercus brandisiana
intro: Quercus brandisiana, also known as the Taiwan oak or Formosan oak, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The leaves and bark of Quercus brandisiana are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Taiwan, for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating cough and sore throat, and supporting liver function. Quercus brandisiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
